articles.name: Creating a Team FCA Profile
articles.title: Creating a Team FCA Profile
articles.body:
To apply for an open staff position with FCA you must create a profile in the Team FCA portal.

Team FCA Portal

FYI

You must have a profile to complete the application process. Your profile will allow you to apply for more than 1 job quickly and easily.

To create a profile, simply begin the application process for a posted job. You will be prompted to create your account. Simply provide your email address and create a password, then click "Create Account":

Attention

Use an email address you have easy and frequent access to! There are many email notifications that guide you through the hiring process. Avoid using school or former job emails addresses that you may lose access to.

After creating your profile, you can complete the initial part of any job posting.

If you progress through the hiring process, you will be prompted at various steps to log into your profile and complete additional steps in the application.

To log back into the Team FCA portal, simply click " Sign In" in the upper right-hand corner and provide the login credentials you had previously created:

articles.name: How to Authorize a Background Check
articles.title: How to Authorize a Background Check
articles.body:
At a certain point in the onboarding process, you must authorize a background check in order to continue as a candidate.

You will be alerted when you must complete the authorization. Follow these instructions to learn how.

Visit the Team FCA portal and click " Sign In" in the upper right-hand corner:

Next, you'll see the "Application Status" for all the jobs you've applied for. The position you must authorize the background check for will be highlighted in red.

Click on the position to proceed to the authorization process. (Note the highlighted text about the background check authorization.)

On the next page, read the instructions carefully and proceed using the blue arrow buttons.

The final page requires your acknowledgment of various disclosures. Simply provide your acknowledgment in the dropdown and click the blue arrow button to proceed:

After completing the authorization, it will take 3-5 business days for the results to return. The Hiring Manager for the position you applied for will receive the results and proceed accordingly.

articles.name: Completing Pre-Onboarding Tasks
articles.title: Completing Pre-Onboarding Tasks
articles.body:
At a certain point in the onboarding process, you must return to the Team FCA portal and complete additional tasks in order to continue as a candidate. These are known as" pre-onboarding tasks."

You will be alerted when you must complete these tasks. Follow these instructions to learn how.

Visit the Team FCA portal and click " Sign In" in the upper right-hand corner:

Next, you'll see the "Application Status" for all the jobs you've applied for. The position you must complete these tasks for will be highlighted in red.

Click on the position to proceed:

First, you must read FCA's Digital Engagement Agreement and acknowledge your consent. Please read the policy in its entirety and sign using the E-Signature at the bottom. Click the blue arrow to proceed:

Next, follow the same steps as above for the Letter of Intent.

Finally, you must complete the Boot Camp Registration form.

Attention

Boot Camp is designed to help develop a healthy, vision-driven, fully funded approach to ministry. Boot Camp is the first step for a Prospective Staff person to be trained on how to successfully fundraise and develop their Home Team.

Boot Camp is required for all new prospective staff.

You can acknowledge that you have already attended Boot Camp by providing the date and location of the training you attended.

Otherwise, you must provide the registration confirmation number for an upcoming training to indicate your forthcoming participation.

Click the blue arrow to proceed to the final "Review and Continue" step where you can finalize these pre-onboarding tasks.

articles.name: Respond to an Offer Letter
articles.title: Respond to an Offer Letter
articles.body:
After you have progressed far enough in the hiring process, gained prospective staff status, and become "fully funded," your Hiring Manager (i.e, the Director) will prepare an offer letter for you to officially join FCA staff.

After the offer letter has gained the necessary approval, you will be notified by email (at the address you provided on your application) when you can review the offer letter.

You must log into the Team FCA portal to review the offer letter and respond to it.

Visit the Team FCA portal and click " Sign In" in the upper right-hand corner:

FYI

You must log in to the profile you created when you first applied (your email will not be your FCA email address).

Next, you'll see the "Application Status" for all the jobs you've applied for. The position you have received an offer will be highlighted in red.

Click on the position to proceed:

On the next page, follow the link to review your offer letter carefully. Then type your full legal name and click "I Accept" or "I Decline" accordingly:

(Confirm your selection in the subsequent pop-up window.)

After accepting an offer, you must complete a final form related to your status as a Minister:

Your Hiring Manager (i.e., your Director) will contact you after they receive your response with the next steps.
